heading: Grants for broadband connectivity
label: 1705 Grants for broadband connectivity

children.heading: Assistant Secretary
children.label: 1 Assistant Secretary
children.text: The term “Assistant Secretary” means the Assistant Secretary of Commerce for Communications and Information.

children.heading: Broadband or broadband service.—
children.label: 2 Broadband or broadband service.—
children.text: The term “broadband” or “broadband service” has the meaning given the term “broadband internet access service” in section 8.1(b) of title 47, Code of Federal Regulations, or any successor regulation.

children.heading: Commission
children.label: 3 Commission
children.text: The term “Commission” means the Federal Communications Commission.

children.heading: Covered broadband project
children.label: 4 Covered broadband project
children.text: The term “covered broadband project” means a competitively and technologically neutral project for the deployment of fixed broadband service that provides qualifying broadband service in an eligible service area.

children.label: A
children.text: a State, or 1 or more political subdivisions of a State; and

children.label: B
children.text: a provider of fixed broadband service.

children.heading: Department
children.label: 6 Department
children.text: The term “Department” means the Department of Commerce.

children.label: A
children.text: the maps created under section 802(c)(1) of the Communications Act of 1934 (); or47 U.S.C. 642(c)(1)

children.label: B
children.text: if the maps described in subparagraph (A) are not available, the most recent information available to the Assistant Secretary, including information provided by the Commission.

children.label: A
children.text: a Tribal Government;

children.label: B
children.text: a Tribal College or University;

children.label: C
children.text: the Department of Hawaiian Home Lands on behalf of the Native Hawaiian Community, including Native Hawaiian Education Programs;

children.label: D
children.text: a Tribal organization; or

children.label: E
children.text: a Native Corporation.

children.heading: Native Corporation
children.label: 9 Native Corporation
children.text: The term “Native Corporation” has the meaning given the term in .section 1602 of title 43

children.heading: Native Hawaiian
children.label: 10 Native Hawaiian
children.text: The term “Native Hawaiian” has the meaning given the term in .section 4221 of title 25

children.label: A
children.text: a download speed of not less than 25 megabits per second;

children.label: B
children.text: an upload speed of not less than 3 megabits per second; and

children.label: C
children.text: a latency sufficient to support real-time, interactive applications.

children.heading: Tribal Government
children.label: 12 Tribal Government
children.text: The term “Tribal Government” means the governing body of any Indian or Alaska Native Tribe, band, nation, pueblo, village, community, component band, or component reservation, individually recognized (including parenthetically) in the list published most recently as of , pursuant to .December 27, 2020section 5131 of title 25

children.label: i
children.text: an Indian reservation, pueblo, or rancheria; or

children.label: ii
children.text: a former reservation within Oklahoma;

children.label: i
children.text: in trust by the United States for the benefit of an Indian Tribe or an individual Indian;

children.label: ii
children.text: by an Indian Tribe or an individual Indian, subject to restriction against alienation under laws of the United States; or

children.label: iii
children.text: by a dependent Indian community;

children.label: C
children.text: any land located within a region established pursuant to ;section 1606(a) of title 43

children.label: D
children.text: Hawaiian Home Lands, as defined in ; orsection 4221 of title 25

children.label: E
children.text: those areas or communities designated by the Assistant Secretary of Indian Affairs of the Department of the Interior that are near, adjacent, or contiguous to reservations where financial assistance and social service programs are provided to Indians because of their status as Indians.

children.label: A
children.text: the household lacks access to qualifying broadband service; and

children.label: B
children.text: no broadband provider has been selected to receive, or is otherwise receiving, Federal or State funding subject to enforceable build out commitments to deploy qualifying broadband service in the specific area where the household is located by dates certain, even if such service is not yet available, provided that the Federal or State agency providing the funding has not deemed the service provider to be in default of its buildout obligations under the applicable Federal or State program.
$1,000,000,000 for grants under subsection (c); and
$300,000,000 for grants under subsection (d).

children.label: A
children.text: broadband service on Tribal land; or

children.label: B
children.text: remote learning, telework, or telehealth resources.

children.heading: Grants
children.label: 2 Grants
children.text: From the amounts appropriated under subsection (b)(1), the Assistant Secretary shall award a grant to each eligible entity that submits an application that the Assistant Secretary approves after consultation with the Commission to prevent duplication of funding.

children.heading: Equitable distribution
children.label: A Equitable distribution
children.text: The amounts appropriated under subsection (b)(1) shall be made available to eligible entities on an equitable basis, and not less than 3 percent of those amounts shall be made available for the benefit of Native Hawaiians.

children.heading: Administrative expenses of Assistant Secretary
children.label: B Administrative expenses of Assistant Secretary
children.text: The Assistant Secretary may use not more than 2 percent of amounts appropriated under subsection (b)(1) for administrative purposes, including the provision of technical assistance to Tribal Governments to help those Governments take advantage of the program established under this subsection.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Not later than 18 months after receiving an allocation of funds pursuant to a specific grant award under this subsection, an eligible entity shall commit the funds in accordance with the approved application of the entity.

children.heading: Reversion of funds
children.label: ii Reversion of funds
children.text: Any grant funds not committed by an eligible entity by the deadline under clause (i) shall be made available to other eligible entities for the purposes provided in this subsection.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Not later than 4 years after receiving an allocation of funds pursuant to a specific grant award under this subsection, an eligible entity shall expend the grant funds.

children.label: I
children.text: the eligible entity has a plan for use of the grant funds;

children.label: II
children.text: the construction project is underway; or

children.label: III
children.text: extenuating circumstances require an extension of time to allow the project to be completed.

children.heading: Extensions for other projects
children.label: iii Extensions for other projects
children.text: The Assistant Secretary may, for good cause shown, extend the period under clause (i) for an eligible entity that proposes to use the grant funds for an eligible use other than construction of broadband infrastructure, based on a detailed showing by the eligible entity of the need for an extension.

children.heading: Reversion of funds
children.label: iv Reversion of funds
children.text: Any grant funds not expended by an eligible entity by the deadline under clause (i) shall be made available to other eligible entities for the purposes provided in this subsection.

children.heading: Multiple grant awards
children.label: C Multiple grant awards
children.text: If the Assistant Secretary awards multiple grants to an eligible entity under this subsection, the deadlines under subparagraphs (A) and (B) shall apply individually to each grant award.

children.label: A
children.text: broadband infrastructure deployment, including support for the establishment of carrier-neutral submarine cable landing stations;

children.label: i
children.text: providing free or reduced-cost broadband service; and

children.label: ii
children.text: preventing disconnection of existing broadband service;

children.label: C
children.text: distance learning;

children.label: D
children.text: telehealth;

children.label: E
children.text: digital inclusion efforts; and

children.label: F
children.text: broadband adoption activities.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), an eligible entity may use not more than 2 percent of grant funds received under this subsection for administrative purposes.

children.heading: Broadband infrastructure projects
children.label: B Broadband infrastructure projects
children.text: An eligible entity that proposes to use grant funds for the construction of broadband infrastructure may use an amount of the grant funds equal to not more than 2.5 percent of the total project cost for planning, feasibility, and sustainability studies related to the project.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: An eligible entity may enter into a contract with a subgrantee, including a non-Tribal entity, as part of its use of grant funds pursuant to this subsection.

children.label: I
children.text: is capable of carrying out the project for which grant funds will be provided in a competent manner in compliance with all applicable laws;

children.label: II
children.text: has the financial capacity to meet the obligations of the project and the requirements of this subsection; and

children.label: III
children.text: has the technical and operational capability to carry out the project; and

children.label: ii
children.text: stipulate in the contract reasonable provisions for recovery of funds for nonperformance.

children.heading: Broadband infrastructure deployment
children.label: 8 Broadband infrastructure deployment
children.text: In using grant funds received under this subsection for new construction of broadband infrastructure, an eligible entity shall prioritize projects that deploy broadband infrastructure to unserved households.

children.heading: Broadband infrastructure deployment grants
children.label: 1 Broadband infrastructure deployment grants
children.text: The Assistant Secretary shall use the funds made available under subsection (b)(2) to implement a program under which the Assistant Secretary makes grants on a competitive basis to covered partnerships for covered broadband projects.

children.heading: Data from commission
children.label: A Data from commission
children.text: Not less frequently than annually, the Commission shall, through the process established under section 802(b)(7)) of the Communications Act of 1934 (), provide the Assistant Secretary any data collected by the Commission pursuant to title VIII of that Act ( et seq.).147 U.S.C. 642(b)(7)47 U.S.C. 641So in original. The second closing parenthesis probably should not appear.1

children.heading: Use by Assistant Secretary
children.label: B Use by Assistant Secretary
children.text: The Assistant Secretary shall rely on the data provided under subparagraph (A) in carrying out this subsection to the greatest extent practicable.

children.label: A
children.text: the covered partnership;

children.label: i
children.text: the speed or speeds at which the covered partnership plans to offer broadband service; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the cost of the project;

children.label: C
children.text: the area to be served by the covered broadband project (in this paragraph referred to as the “proposed service area”);

children.label: i
children.text: any grant, loan, or loan guarantee provided by a State to the provider of broadband service for the deployment of broadband service in the proposed service area;

children.label: aa
children.text: any program to provide grants, loans, or loan guarantees under sections 601 through 603 of that Act ( et seq.); and7 U.S.C. 950bb

children.label: bb
children.text: the Community Connect Grant Program established under section 604 of that Act (); or7 U.S.C. 950bb–3

children.label: II
children.text: the broadband loan and grant pilot program known as the “Rural eConnectivity Pilot Program” or the “ReConnect Program” authorized under section 779 of division A of the Consolidated Appropriations Act, 2018 (; );Public Law 115–141132 Stat. 348

children.label: iii
children.text: any high-cost universal service support provided under section 254 of the Communications Act of 1934 ();47 U.S.C. 254

children.label: iv
children.text: any grant provided under ;section 1305 of this title

children.label: v
children.text: amounts made available for the Education Stabilization Fund under the heading “DEPARTMENT OF EDUCATION” in title VIII of division B of the CARES Act (; ); orPublic Law 116–136134 Stat. 564

children.label: vi
children.text: any other grant, loan, or loan guarantee provided by the Federal Government for the provision of broadband service.

children.label: A
children.text: Covered broadband projects designed to provide broadband service to the greatest number of households in an eligible service area.

children.label: i
children.text: a county, city, or town that has a population of more than 50,000 inhabitants; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the urbanized area contiguous and adjacent to a city or town described in clause (i).

children.label: C
children.text: Covered broadband projects that are the most cost-effective, prioritizing such projects in areas that are the most rural.

children.label: D
children.text: Covered broadband projects designed to provide broadband service with a download speed of not less than 100 megabits per second and an upload speed of not less than 20 megabits per second.

children.label: E
children.text: Any other covered broadband project that meets the requirements of this subsection.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Not later than 1 year after receiving grant funds under this subsection, a covered partnership shall expend the grant funds.

children.label: i
children.text: the covered partnership has a plan for use of the grant funds;

children.label: ii
children.text: the construction project is underway; or

children.label: iii
children.text: extenuating circumstances require an extension of time to allow the project to be completed.

children.heading: Reversion of funds
children.label: C Reversion of funds
children.text: Any grant funds not expended by an covered partnership by the deadline under subparagraph (A) shall be made available to other covered partnerships for the purposes provided in this subsection.

children.label: i
children.text: from using the grant amounts to repay, or make any other payment relating to, a loan made by any public or private lender;

children.label: ii
children.text: from using grant amounts as collateral for a loan made by any public or private lender; and

children.label: iii
children.text: from using more than $50,000 of the grant amounts to pay for the preparation of the grant.

children.heading: Nondiscrimination
children.label: B Nondiscrimination
children.text: The Assistant Secretary may not require a provider of broadband service that is part of a covered partnership to be designated as an eligible telecommunications carrier pursuant to section 214(e) of the Communications Act of 1934 () to be eligible to receive a grant under this subsection or as a condition of receiving a grant under this subsection.47 U.S.C. 214(e)

children.label: A
children.text: issue a notice inviting eligible entities and covered partnerships to submit applications for grants under this section, which shall contain details about how awarding decisions will be made; and

children.label: i
children.text: the requirements for applications for grants under this section; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the allowed uses of grant funds awarded under this section.

children.heading: Submission
children.label: A Submission
children.text: During the 90-day period beginning on the date on which the Assistant Secretary issues the notice under paragraph (1), an eligible entity or covered partnership may submit an application for a grant under this section.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Not later than 90 days after receiving an application under subparagraph (A), the Assistant Secretary shall approve or deny the application.

children.label: I
children.text: the Assistant Secretary provides the applicant an opportunity to cure any defects in the application; and

children.label: II
children.text: after receiving the opportunity under subclause (I), the applicant still fails to meet the requirements of this section.

children.heading: Single application
children.label: C Single application
children.text: An eligible entity or covered partnership may submit only 1 application under this paragraph.

children.heading: Proposed use of funds
children.label: D Proposed use of funds
children.text: An application submitted by an eligible entity or a covered partnership under this paragraph shall describe each proposed use of grant funds.

children.heading: Allocation of funds
children.label: E Allocation of funds
children.text: Not later than 14 days after approving an application for a grant under this paragraph, the Assistant Secretary shall allocate the grant funds to the eligible entity or covered partnership.

children.label: I
children.text: the eligible entity under subsection (c) available to other eligible entities on an equitable basis; or

children.label: II
children.text: the covered partnership under subsection (d) to other covered partnerships.

children.heading: Second process
children.label: ii Second process
children.text: The Assistant Secretary shall initiate a second notice and application process described in this subsection to reallocate any funds made available to other eligible entities or covered partnerships under clause (i).

children.label: A
children.text: ensure sufficient transparency, accountability, and oversight to provide the public with information regarding the award and use of grant funds under this section;

children.label: B
children.text: ensure that a recipient of a grant under this section uses the grant funds in compliance with the requirements of this section and the overall purpose of the applicable grant program under this section; and

children.label: C
children.text: deter waste, fraud, and abuse of grant funds.

children.heading: Prohibition on use for covered communications equipment or services
children.label: 4 Prohibition on use for covered communications equipment or services
children.text: An eligible entity or covered partnership may not use grant funds received under this section to purchase or support any covered communications equipment or service (as defined in ).section 1608 of this title

children.heading: Unauthorized use of funds
children.label: 5 Unauthorized use of funds
children.text: To the extent that the Assistant Secretary or the Inspector General of the Department determines that an eligible entity or covered partnership has expended grant funds received under this section in violation of this section, the Assistant Secretary shall recover the amount of funds that were so expended.

children.heading: Additional appropriations for Tribal Broadband Connectivity Program
children.label: 6 Additional appropriations for Tribal Broadband Connectivity Program
i
children.text: means the allocation under paragraph (2)(E) of funds appropriated under subsection (b)(1); and

children.label: ii
children.text: does not include any reallocation of funds under paragraph (2)(F).

children.label: i
children.text: may use a portion of the funds to fully fund any grants under that subsection for which the Assistant Secretary received an application and which the Assistant Secretary did not fully fund during the initial round of funding; and

children.label: ii
children.text: shall allocate any remaining funds through subsequent funding rounds consistent with the requirements of this section, except as provided in subparagraph (C) of this paragraph.

children.label: i
children.text: the Assistant Secretary shall not be required to issue an additional notice under paragraph (1) of this subsection, but shall inform eligible entities that additional funding has been made available for grants under subsection (c) and describe the changes made to the Tribal Broadband Connectivity Program under that subsection by section 60201 of the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act;

children.label: ii
children.text: the requirement under paragraph (2)(C) of this subsection shall be applied individually to each round of funding for grants under subsection (c);

children.label: iii
children.text: paragraph (2)(A) of this subsection shall be applied by substituting “180-day period beginning on the date on which the Assistant Secretary informs eligible entities that additional funding has been made available for grants under subsection (c)” for “90-day period beginning on the date on which the Assistant Secretary issues the notice under paragraph (1)”; and

children.label: iv
children.text: notwithstanding paragraph (2)(F) of this subsection, in the case of funds appropriated under subsection (b)(1) that were not allocated during the initial round of funding, the Assistant Secretary may elect to allocate the funds during any subsequent round of funding for grants under subsection (c).

children.label: i
children.text: describes how the eligible entity or covered partnership expended the funds;

children.label: I
children.text: a description of each service provided with the grant funds; and

children.label: II
children.text: the number of locations or geographic areas at which broadband service was provided using the grant funds; and

children.label: iii
children.text: identifies each subgrantee that received a subgrant from the eligible entity or covered partnership and a description of the specific project for which grant funds were provided.

children.heading: Provision of information to FCC and USDA
children.label: B Provision of information to FCC and USDA
children.text: The Assistant Secretary shall provide the information collected under subparagraph (A) to the Commission and the Department of Agriculture to be used when determining whether to award funds for the deployment of broadband under any program administered by those agencies.

children.heading: Transmission of reports to Congress
children.label: C Transmission of reports to Congress
children.text: Not later than 5 days after receiving a report from an eligible entity under subparagraph (A), the Assistant Secretary shall transmit the report to the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation of the Senate and the Committee on Energy and Commerce of the House of Representatives.

children.heading: Inspector General and GAO
children.label: 2 Inspector General and GAO
children.text: Not later than 6 months after the date on which the first grant is awarded under this section, and every 6 months thereafter until all of the grant funds awarded under this section are expended, the Inspector General of the Department and the Comptroller General of the United States shall each submit to the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation of the Senate and the Committee on Energy and Commerce of the House of Representatives a report that reviews the grants awarded under this section during the preceding 6-month period. Each such report shall include recommendations to address waste, fraud, and abuse, if any.

children.heading: Impact on other Federal broadband programs
children.label: g Impact on other Federal broadband programs
children.text: The use of grant funds received under this section by an eligible entity, covered partnership, or subgrantee shall not impact the eligibility of, or otherwise disadvantage, the eligible entity, covered partnership, or subgrantee with respect to participation in any other Federal broadband program.
